message:
feat(membrane): major runtime math expansion (+346 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -427,7 +427,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-d5l","title":"Perf: Microbench RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe (criterion + ns/op)","description":"Create a repeatable benchmark that measures runtime_math overhead directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bench measures decide() and observe_validation_result() separately and together.
- Reports p50/p95/p99 ns/op and throughput.
- Produces stable output suitable for regression gating.

Implementation notes:
- Keep benchmark deterministic: fixed inputs, fixed warmup, pin thread if needed.
- Include both strict and hardened mode paths.
- Record compiler flags / CPU model in output metadata.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.703138634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254231420Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254213887Z","close_reason":"bench implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1081,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented criterion microbench  (decide / observe_validation_result / decide+observe) in  and wired it in .

How to run:
- Strict: RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META glibc_rust_mode_env=strict
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META rustflags=<unset>
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META cpu_model=: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5995WX 64-Cores
runtime_math/decide/strict
                        time:   [1.0968 µs 1.1060 µs 1.1172 µs]
                        thrpt:  [895.13 Kelem/s 904.19 Kelem/s 911.75 Kelem/s]
                 change:
                        time:   [-1.9912% +0.0240% +2.0613%] (p = 0.99 > 0.05)
                        thrpt:  [-2.0197% -0.0240% +2.0316%]
                        No change in performance detected.
Found 16 outliers among 100 measurements (16.00%)
  2 (2.00%) high mild
  14 (14.00%) high severe

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=strict bench=decide samples=110 p50_ns_op=1093.831 p95_ns_op=1637.438 p99_ns_op=1727.887 mean_ns_op=1186.927 throughput_ops_s=901560.862
runtime_math/observe_fast/strict
                        time:   [3.4119 µs 3.4687 µs 3.5429 µs]
                        thrpt:  [282.25 Kelem/s 288.29 Kelem/s 293.09 Kelem/s]
                 change:
                        time:   [-5.3607% -0.0676% +5.4778%] (p = 0.98 > 0.05)
                        thrpt:  [-5.1934% +0.0677% +5.6644%]
                        No change in performance detected.
Found 7 outliers among 100 measurements (7.00%)
  7 (7.00%) high mild

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=strict bench=observe_fast samples=108 p50_ns_op=3539.561 p95_ns_op=5505.000 p99_ns_op=6209.688 mean_ns_op=3984.990 throughput_ops_s=282152.298
runtime_math/decide_observe/strict
                        time:   [2.7311 µs 2.7536 µs 2.7813 µs]
                        thrpt:  [359.54 Kelem/s 363.16 Kelem/s 366.15 Kelem/s]
                 change:
                        time:   [-16.076% -13.607% -11.118%] (p = 0.00 < 0.05)
                        thrpt:  [+12.509% +15.751% +19.156%]
                        Performance has improved.
Found 9 outliers among 100 measurements (9.00%)
  7 (7.00%) high mild
  2 (2.00%) high severe

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=strict bench=decide_observe samples=109 p50_ns_op=2766.755 p95_ns_op=3600.642 p99_ns_op=3988.336 mean_ns_op=2900.411 throughput_ops_s=361242.763
- Hardened: RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META glibc_rust_mode_env=hardened
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META rustflags=<unset>
RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META cpu_model=: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5995WX 64-Cores
runtime_math/decide/hardened
                        time:   [950.45 ns 956.86 ns 964.71 ns]
                        thrpt:  [1.0366 Melem/s 1.0451 Melem/s 1.0521 Melem/s]
Found 8 outliers among 100 measurements (8.00%)
  6 (6.00%) high mild
  2 (2.00%) high severe

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=hardened bench=decide samples=111 p50_ns_op=946.522 p95_ns_op=1015.690 p99_ns_op=1053.307 mean_ns_op=955.131 throughput_ops_s=1044978.944
runtime_math/observe_fast/hardened
                        time:   [3.3547 µs 3.3778 µs 3.4073 µs]
                        thrpt:  [293.49 Kelem/s 296.05 Kelem/s 298.09 Kelem/s]
Found 12 outliers among 100 measurements (12.00%)
  12 (12.00%) high mild

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=hardened bench=observe_fast samples=108 p50_ns_op=3453.237 p95_ns_op=5449.906 p99_ns_op=5991.000 mean_ns_op=3873.269 throughput_ops_s=289938.314
runtime_math/decide_observe/hardened
                        time:   [4.3028 µs 4.5118 µs 4.7518 µs]
                        thrpt:  [210.44 Kelem/s 221.64 Kelem/s 232.41 Kelem/s]
Found 5 outliers among 100 measurements (5.00%)
  5 (5.00%) high mild

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH mode=hardened bench=decide_observe samples=108 p50_ns_op=4354.797 p95_ns_op=6938.384 p99_ns_op=8046.975 mean_ns_op=4767.682 throughput_ops_s=218937.764

Output:
- Prints  (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, /proc/cpuinfo model).
- Prints  per benchmark.

Determinism knobs:
- Fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx).
- Fixed warmup loops (10k iters) before measurement.
- Optional Linux CPU pinning via .","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:09:18Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"NOTE: Previous comment accidentally expanded benchmark output due to shell backticks; ignore it for instructions.

What changed:
- Added crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_bench.rs
- Added [[bench]] runtime_math_bench entry in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml

Bench targets:
- runtime_math/decide/<mode>
- runtime_math/observe_fast/<mode>
- runtime_math/decide_observe/<mode>

How to run:
- Strict: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench
- Hardened: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=hardened cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench

Output:
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META lines (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, CPU model)
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH lines with p50/p95/p99/mean ns/op and throughput ops/s

Determinism:
- fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx)
- fixed warmup loops (10k iters)
- optional Linux CPU pinning: GLIBC_RUST_BENCH_PIN=1","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dfe","title":"EPIC: Inventory + drift control (AGENTS.md vs runtime_math code; snapshot schema stability)","description":"Goal: prevent architectural drift.

Why:
- runtime_math has many moving parts; if AGENTS.md lists required modules/decision law but code diverges, future work becomes inconsistent.

Outputs:
- A repeatable check that module inventory + decision law in AGENTS.md matches code.
- A stable, versioned RuntimeKernelSnapshot schema (new fields are additive and documented).
- Fusion signal vector size always matches number of cached states.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.669320398Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43.636260651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:36.812282675Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1az","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1tx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-1wy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-29r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-31i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-ju7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dfe","depends_on_id":"bd-p5i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Inventory + Drift Control

### Why We Need This
This repo is intentionally ambitious: lots of runtime_math modules and many moving parts. Drift is the default failure mode.

We must continuously ensure:
- AGENTS.md module inventory matches code reality
- decide() implementation matches the documented decision law
- snapshot schemas are stable and versioned
- fused signals are consistent across modules

### What This Epic Produces
- automated checks (compile-time + tests) that fail loudly on drift
- a wiring checklist so adding kernels doesn’t silently omit snapshots/tests
- conservative merge rules for decision-law changes (behavior proofs required)

### Definition Of Done
- We can answer: “why did runtime choose FullValidate here?” with a stable policy_id + evidence.
- A new runtime_math module can’t land without:
  - being registered
  - being snapshotted
  - being represented in fusion signals (if relevant)
  - being covered by at least one test","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:17Z"},{"id":1084,"issue_id":"bd-dfe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 7 blockers CLOSED. check_module_inventory.sh (68/68 in sync), check_module_wiring.sh, check_snapshot_coverage.sh (68/68 covered), snapshot_gate.sh, perf_gate.sh all passing. Schema versioning, cadence classification, fusion consistency, decision-law validation all delivered.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:13:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ef2","title":"I/O migration wave: route core unistd/io ABI entrypoints through raw veneer","description":"Critique mapping: #2.

Deliverables:
- Migrate open/close/read/write/pread64/pwrite64/lseek/fcntl subset used by smoke suite.
- Preserve strict mode ABI semantics and hardened mode evidence emission.

Acceptance:
- LD_PRELOAD smoke suite passes with migration enabled.
- Compatibility fixtures show no semantic drift on return values/errno.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.198369909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:20:06.228805Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","io","syscall"]}
-{"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:23:49.637290408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/alien_cs_metrics.rs` to import the local fallback `DecisionId` and `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION`.
- Metric event JSONL rows now emit `decision_id` and `schema_version`.
- Aggregate snapshot JSONL rows now emit `decision_id: 0` and `schema_version`.
- Tightened the Alien CS JSONL tests to require those fields and to assert deterministic values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture`
  Expected: alien_cs_metrics targeted tests pass with the updated JSONL contract.
  Result: passed, 14 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- `bv --robot-triage/--robot-next/--robot-insights` still ranks the TSM epic as the strongest actionable lane. This bead was the next concrete, useful step because it extends canonical fallback IDs into another structured evidence exporter without waiting for full `franken_kernel` integration.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project currently shows only `MistyGrove` registered, so there were no peer agents to notify directly.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:42:31Z"},{"id":1294,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Added a second `bd-epeg` slice by aligning the HJI proof artifact schema tag with the shared fallback membrane schema.

Changes:
- Updated `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/hji_reachability.rs` to use `MEMBRANE_SCHEMA_VERSION.to_string()` instead of a one-off `"v1"` literal.
- Tightened `viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts` to assert `schema_version == "1.0"`.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane viability_artifact_reports_expected_kernel_counts -- --nocapture`
  Expected: HJI artifact test passes with shared schema version.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane proof_adversary_witnesses_are_sound -- --nocapture`
  Expected: witness proof remains valid after schema change.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- This continues the same actionable `bd-epeg` lane chosen from `bv --robot-*`: move remaining structured/proof artifacts onto the shared fallback schema instead of isolated local version strings.
- Agent Mail remained empty and no other agents are registered in this project, so there were no direct coordination messages to send or answer.","created_at":"2026-03-15T15:46:51Z"},{"id":1295,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting another bounded bd-epeg slice: aligning runtime_math/evidence JSON export with the shared fallback ID/schema contract. Reserving `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` now and will verify with targeted rch cargo check/test once patched.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:11:34Z"},{"id":1296,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

Changes:
- Added the shared fallback membrane `schema_version` field to `export_decision_cards_json()` while preserving the existing `schema: "decision_cards.v1"` contract.
- Routed exported `decision_id` and `policy_id` through the canonical local wrappers (`DecisionId`, `PolicyId`) before serialization, keeping the standalone adoption wedge intact.
- Tightened `decision_card_export_is_valid_json` to require `schema_version == "1.0"` and positive exported `decision_id`/`policy_id` values.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed (remote exit=0; artifact retrieval ended with rsync code-24 vanished-file warning after success).
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_export_is_valid_json -- --nocapture`
  Expected: export contract test passes with shared schema version.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_query_filters_by_type_thread_symbol_and_export -- --nocapture`
  Expected: decision-card query/export regression still passes.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- Chosen from the same `bv --robot-*` membrane lane; this continues moving remaining runtime-math evidence exporters onto the shared fallback kernel-style ID/schema contract.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project still only shows `MistyGrove` registered.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:13:19Z"},{"id":1297,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded `bd-epeg` slice: extending the shared fallback ID/schema contract deeper into `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`, this time on the evidence-symbol export side. Reserving the file now and will verify via targeted rch cargo check/test after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:30:13Z"},{"id":1298,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented another bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`.

Changes:
- Extended `export_decision_cards_json()` to emit canonical scoped `trace_id` values using the local fallback `DecisionId::scoped_trace_id("runtime_math::decision_card")`.
- Preserved the existing `decision_cards.v1` export schema while keeping the shared `schema_version = "1.0"` contract already added in the prior slice.
- Tightened `decision_card_export_is_valid_json` so every exported card must include a `trace_id` with the canonical `runtime_math::decision_card::` prefix.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib`
  Expected: compile success with exit 0.
  Result: passed (remote exit=0; artifact retrieval ended with an rsync code-24 vanished-file warning after the successful compile).
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_export_is_valid_json -- --nocapture`
  Expected: decision-card export JSON contract passes with canonical trace IDs.

- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane decision_card_query_filters_by_type_thread_symbol_and_export -- --nocapture`
  Expected: query/export regression still passes after adding trace IDs.
  Result: passed, 1 passed / 0 failed.

Notes:
- Chosen from the same `bv --robot-*` actionable membrane lane; this continues moving runtime evidence exports onto the shared fallback kernel-style ID contract instead of leaving ad hoc fields in downstream tooling.
- Agent Mail inbox remained empty and the project still only shows `MistyGrove` registered.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:32:21Z"},{"id":1299,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting the next bounded `bd-epeg` slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs`: enriching the decision-card JSON export with the higher-level canonical evidence fields still missing from this path (`api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `artifact_refs`, and mode label coverage) while preserving the existing stable schema. Reserving the file now and will verify via targeted rch check/tests after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-15T20:57:45Z"},{"id":1300,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"MistyGrove","text":"Continued bd-epeg after bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next kept the membrane lane as the strongest actionable path. I’m reserving that file now and will verify through targeted rch cargo check/tests after patching.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:38:09Z"},{"id":1302,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"MistyGrove","text":"Continued bd-epeg after bv --format json --robot-triage / --robot-next kept the membrane lane as the strongest actionable path. This covers decision rows (dispatch / decision / evidence) and non-decision rows (pressure, overload policy, reverse-round, calibration, snapshot, certificate, regret, drift). Tightened the JSONL tests to require those canonical prefixes. Verification via rch passed: rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane alien_cs_metrics -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-15T21:54:07Z"},{"id":1305,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Continued the next bounded canonical-evidence slice in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/ptr_validator.rs by tightening the structured-log regression to require the canonical trace_id prefix `tsm::pointer_validation::` instead of only checking for a generic `::` separator. +{"id":"bd-epeg","title":"Cross-cutting: franken_kernel crate integration (Cx, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId)","description":"Integrate FrankenLibC with franken_kernel suite substrate: adopt canonical Cx capability context, TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types. All TSM decisions must flow through Cx. All evidence records must use canonical IDs. Must not fork franken_kernel schema. ADOPTION WEDGE: FrankenLibC must function standalone without franken_kernel (fallback to local types).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:16.769752763Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T13:06:19.654515277Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adoption-wedge","cross-crate","franken-kernel","frankenlibc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-32e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-epeg","depends_on_id":"bd-oai","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1293,"issue_id":"bd-epeg","author":"ubuntu","text":"Started a bounded `bd-epeg` slice by extending the local fallback ID/schema contract into `alien_cs_metrics`.

Verified scripts/update_conformance_golden.sh, scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh, scripts/update_golden_snapshots.sh, scripts/snapshot_gate.sh, and CI ordering in scripts/ci.sh (golden gates run before perf gate under GLIBC_RUST_EXTENDED_GATES=1). Ran: scripts/conformance_golden_gate.sh (PASS), scripts/snapshot_gate.sh (PASS), cargo fmt --check (PASS), cargo check -p glibc-rs-harness (PASS), cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness (PASS).","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:10:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-g7ux","title":"inet_addr returns host-order u32 instead of network-order","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:37.756487821Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384420744Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:27.384352917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","inet"],"comments":[{"id":1086,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"frankenlibc_core::inet::inet_addr uses u32::from_be_bytes(octets) which returns the host-endian integer value (0x7F000001 = 2130706433 for 127.0.0.1). glibc inet_addr returns the same 4 bytes stored as a native u32 in network byte order (0x0100007F = 16777343 on LE). Fix: change from_be_bytes to from_ne_bytes in crates/frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Detected by conformance differential execution.","created_at":"2026-02-13T10:10:45Z"},{"id":1087,"issue_id":"bd-g7ux","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Fixed inet_addr to return network byte order. Changed u32::from_be_bytes to u32::from_ne_bytes in frankenlibc-core/src/inet/mod.rs:63. Updated 5 tests that incorrectly used to_be_bytes() to use to_ne_bytes(). Also fixed pre-existing pthread compilation error: added tls_values field to ThreadHandle and wired up register_thread_tls call with the correct 2-arg signature. All 61 inet tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:27:26Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-gcy","title":"bd-c1x subtask: Futex condvar core implementation (wait/signal/broadcast/timedwait)","description":"Background:
- Condvar implementation must coordinate with mutex ownership and futex wake ordering safely.

Goal:
- Implement futex-based condvar core with deterministic sequence and wake semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Internal condvar sequence/counter design.
2) wait path with unlock/relock choreography around futex_wait.
3) signal/broadcast wake policy.
4) Timed wait with absolute deadline handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Core condvar operations satisfy spec for success/error/timeout cases.
- No missed wakeups in deterministic stress tests.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests: signal-before-wait, wait-before-signal, broadcast-many, timeout races.
- Structured logs: trace_id, mode, condvar_state, mutex_state, wake_count, timeout_state, errno.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHawk","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:00:51.531693883Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.207008001Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:32.206927771Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","futex","implementation","pthread","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1088,"issue_id":"bd-gcy","author":"PurpleHawk","text":"Completed futex condvar core implementation (bd-gcy). Deliverables:

1) CondvarData repr(C) struct with seq counter, waiter count, associated mutex, and clock_id — overlays onto pthread_cond_t memory (20 bytes, fits in 48-byte struct).

2) condvar_wait(): atomic unlock-wait-relock choreography using sys_futex. Captures seq before unlock, parks with FUTEX_WAIT on seq word, relocks via CAS+futex loop on return. Validates mutex association invariant (EINVAL on mismatch).

3) condvar_timedwait(): uses FUTEX_WAIT_BITSET with absolute timeout. FUTEX_CLOCK_REALTIME flag selects clock domain matching condvar's configured clock. Returns ETIMEDOUT on deadline expiry after relocking mutex (per POSIX).

4) condvar_signal(): increments seq + futex_wake(1). condvar_broadcast(): increments seq + futex_wake(INT_MAX). Both are no-ops when no waiters (seq still incremented for consistency).

5) relock_mutex() helper: simple CAS loop with futex parking for relocking after condvar wait.

6) 64 total tests pass: 41 contract tests (bd-blg) + 23 core implementation tests including 4 threaded integration tests:
   - core_condvar_wait_signal_roundtrip (single waiter + signal)
   - core_condvar_broadcast_wakes_all (4 waiters + broadcast)
   - core_condvar_timedwait_expires (past deadline -> ETIMEDOUT)
   - core_condvar_mutex_association_mismatch (EINVAL on different mutex)

7) Exports from pthread module: CondvarData, condvar_init/destroy/wait/timedwait/signal/broadcast.

Verification: cargo test -p frankenlibc-core -- pthread::cond (64 passed, 0 failed). cargo check --all-targets clean.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:26Z"}]}
